title: Identification of Long Non-coding RNAs Involved in Floral Scent of Rose (Rosa hybrida)
author: Shaochuan Shi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Supplementary material for the article, including the followings: 

  1. Supplementary Figures  

 Figure S1. Q-PCR validation of selected lncRNAs expression across flowering stages in rose ‘Tianmidemeng’.   Expression levels of selected transcripts measured by Q-PCR and RNA-Seq are showed in the same histograms. Black columns indicate relative gene expression levels detected by Q-PCR (left y-axis; normalized units). Grey columns represent expression determined by RNA-Seq in RPKM units (right y-axis). 

 Figure S2. Associations between module and floral volatiles by WGCNA analysis. Each row corresponds to a module eigengene, column to a trait. Each cell contains the corresponding correlation and p-value. The table is color-coded by correlation according to the color legend. 

  2. Supplementary Dataset   (uploaded as excel file)  

 Table S1 | Primers used in Q-PCR validation for RNA-seq data. 

 Table S2 | Floral volatile changes during flower development process in rose 'Tianmidemeng'. 

 Table S3 | List of lncRNAs in rose. 

 Table S4 | Differentially-expressed lncRNAs during flower development in rose 'Tianmidemeng'. 

 Table S5 | Predicted target genes for lncRNAs in rose 'Tianmidemeng'. 

 Table S6 | Correlated lncRNAs to genes involved in downstream pathways of floral scent synthesis in rose. 

 Table S7 | Correlated lncRNAs to genes involved in upstream pathways of floral scent synthesis in rose. 

 Table S8 | Correlated lncRNAs to transcription factors involved in floral scent synthesis in rose. 

 Table S9 | LncRNAs with expression levels involved in the modules.
